steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The original map side is 40 inches, and 1 inch represents 18 miles. The full 40-inch side represents 40 \times 18 = 720 actual miles.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The smaller map is 60% shorter, so it keeps only 40% of the original side length: 40 \times 0.40 = 16 inches. That same 720 actual miles is now compressed into 16 inches.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The new scale is 720 \div 16 = 45 miles per inch.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• A: 7.20 — This is less than the original 18 miles per inch, but a shorter map must represent more miles per inch, not fewer. Eliminated.
  • B: 11.25 — Also less than 18; same reasoning rules this out. Eliminated.
  • C: 24.00 — Larger than 18 but far smaller than 720 ÷ 16. Eliminated.
  • D: 45.00 — 720 ÷ 16 = 45. D is correct.
